visa_types.name: Lesotho Exemption Permit
visa_types.documents_required:
- Applicants are requested to get original documents for verification while submitting photocopies for the permit
- If you have completed the online form and taken an appointment through the website then you need to carry the duly completed and signed online form with you to the center. Handwritten
                                                                            forms will not be accepted by Department of Home Affairs.
- Copy of valid Lesotho passport *Original Lesotho Passport is required while submitting the application for verification.
- Certified copy of previous exemption permit
- A copy of the Lesotho Birth Certificate or the Lesotho ID Card. (All applicants need to be registered on the National Population Register of Leostho before applying for the Lesotho
                                                                            Special Permit)
- Print out of Online Payment Reciept or Original Bank payment receipt for each applicant (if a cash deposit prepayment is done in our Standard Bank account)
- Letter of your Appointment to submit at the VFS centre
- As per the permit category you apply for below documents will be required

[Table:  Lesotho Exemption Permit]

- * It is recommended that the passport be valid for a period of more than four years. If the validity period of the passport or the travel document is less than four years, the holder will bear the cost of the replacement of both the passport and the LEP into the new passport
visa_types.visa_fees:
The fees are mentioned below
A fixed service fee of R1550 for every applicant
A biometric clearance fee of R185 (Applicable to all who are 18 years and above)
- All adults (18years and over) will pay a total of R1735
- All minors (below 18years of age) will pay a total of R1550
Fees are non-refundable once the application is received by VFS Global and Department of Home Affairs.
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/kVs59OYSSFsA344Mlzm4R/24c575562a2217cb0d9b843b047812d2/Discliamer_privcay_060316.pdf) to read the terms and conditions of payment
visa_types.processing_time:
All applications accepted at the Application Centre will have an average processing time of 8 weeks (approximate timeline) for the decision to be returned from Department of Home Affairs.
You can track your application online on VFS Global website to determine status of your application.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
 The special dispensation is for Lesotho nationals who are working, studying or running businesses in The Republic of South Africa and have been in the country in such capacity before
                                                                        30 September 2015
The Lesotho Exemption Permit (LEP) is a permit issued by the Department of Home Affairs, South Africa.
Lesotho Citizens that would like to remain in South Africa can apply for the newly introduced Lesotho Exemption Permit (LEP) provided they have a valid LEP permit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
 The special dispensation is for Lesotho nationals who are working, studying or running businesses in The Republic of South Africa and have been in the country in such capacity before
                                                                        30 September 2015
The Lesotho Exemption Permit (LEP) is a permit issued by the Department of Home Affairs, South Africa.
Lesotho Citizens that would like to remain in South Africa can apply for the newly introduced Lesotho Exemption Permit (LEP) provided they have a valid LEP permit. 

## Fees
The fees are mentioned below
A fixed service fee of R1550 for every applicant
A biometric clearance fee of R185 (Applicable to all who are 18 years and above)
- All adults (18years and over) will pay a total of R1735
- All minors (below 18years of age) will pay a total of R1550
Fees are non-refundable once the application is received by VFS Global and Department of Home Affairs.
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/kVs59OYSSFsA344Mlzm4R/24c575562a2217cb0d9b843b047812d2/Discliamer_privcay_060316.pdf) to read the terms and conditions of payment

## Documents Required
- Applicants are requested to get original documents for verification while submitting photocopies for the permit
- If you have completed the online form and taken an appointment through the website then you need to carry the duly completed and signed online form with you to the center. Handwritten
                                                                            forms will not be accepted by Department of Home Affairs.
- Copy of valid Lesotho passport *Original Lesotho Passport is required while submitting the application for verification.
- Certified copy of previous exemption permit
- A copy of the Lesotho Birth Certificate or the Lesotho ID Card. (All applicants need to be registered on the National Population Register of Leostho before applying for the Lesotho
                                                                            Special Permit)
- Print out of Online Payment Reciept or Original Bank payment receipt for each applicant (if a cash deposit prepayment is done in our Standard Bank account)
- Letter of your Appointment to submit at the VFS centre
- As per the permit category you apply for below documents will be required

[Table:  Lesotho Exemption Permit]

- * It is recommended that the passport be valid for a period of more than four years. If the validity period of the passport or the travel document is less than four years, the holder will bear the cost of the replacement of both the passport and the LEP into the new passport

## Processing Time
 All applications accepted at the Application Centre will have an average processing time of 8 weeks (approximate timeline) for the decision to be returned from Department of Home Affairs.
You can track your application online on VFS Global website to determine status of your application.

## Online Forms
Please click here (https://www.vfsvisaonline.com/DhaLesothoOnlineVaf/Login.aspx) to complete your online form.
